---
title: "Data subsampling"
author: "Camila Diaz"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.



```{r, echo=FALSE}

sf_data1 <- all_data |>
  filter(!Species == "") |>
  filter(!Genus == "") |>
  mutate(Observer = iconv(Observer,
                      from = "latin1",
                      to   = "UTF-8"),
         Observer = na_if(Observer, ""),
         Observer = if_else(is.na(Observer), "Unknown", Observer)) |>
  mutate(Observer = stri_trans_general(Observer, "Latin-ASCII")) |>
  mutate(Date.Observed = as.Date(Date.Observed)) |>
  mutate(Species_name = paste0(Genus, " ", Species)) |>
  mutate(
    start_time_parsed = parse_date_time( #Convert to 0–23 hour scale
      Start.time,
      orders = c("H:M", "H:M:S", "I:M p", "I p")),
    start_hour = hour(start_time_parsed)) |>
  select("OccuranceID", 
           "Species_name", 
           "Number.of.individuals", 
           "Site.ID", 
           "Checklist.ID",
           "Latitude",
           "Longitude",
           "Distance.Traveled",
           "Date.Observed",
           "Start.time",
           "start_hour",
           "Duration",
           "Sampling.protocol",
           "Party.Size",
           "Basis.of.record",
           "Observer",
           "Status",
           "Checklist.Completed"
    )

write.csv(sf_data1, "C:/Users/maria/Documents/PhD/data/species/records_clean.csv")
# Check structure
str(sf_data1)


```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
obs_tbl <- sf_data1 %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(Observer) %>%
  summarise(
    checklist_count = n_distinct(Checklist.ID),
    species_count   = n_distinct(Species_name),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    logC = log1p(checklist_count),
    logS = log1p(species_count),
    # standardize to mean 0 sd 1 for Stan stability
    logC_z = as.numeric(scale(logC)),
    logS_z = as.numeric(scale(logS))
  )

write.csv(obs_tbl, "C:/Users/maria/Documents/PhD/data/species/Observers.csv")
```


Variable classes (numeric, character, factor, Date, etc.)
```{r, echo=FALSE}
all_data <- read.delim(
  "C:/Users/maria/Downloads/eButterfly_surveys/occurrence.txt",
  header = TRUE,
  fill = TRUE,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
colnames(all_data)

sf_data1 <- all_data |>
  mutate(
    # Checklist completeness
    Checklist.Completed = case_when(
      str_detect(samplingEffort, "^Complete checklist")   ~ "Yes",
      str_detect(samplingEffort, "^Incomplete checklist") ~ "No",
      TRUE                                                 ~ ""
    ),

    # Party size (extract first integer found)
    Party.Size = str_extract(samplingEffort, "//d+") |>
      as.integer()
  ) |>
  filter(!species == "") |>
  mutate(#recordedBy = iconv(recordedBy,
                      #from = "latin1",
                      #to   = "UTF-8"),
         recordedBy = na_if(recordedBy, ""),
         recordedBy = if_else(is.na(recordedBy), "Unknown", recordedBy),
         recordedBy = stri_trans_general(recordedBy, "Latin-ASCII")
         ) |>
  mutate(
    OccuranceID = str_extract(occurrenceID, "//d+") |>
      as.integer(),
    Species_name = species,
    Number.of.individuals = individualCount,
    Site.ID = str_extract(eventID, "//d+") |>
      as.integer(),
    Checklist.ID = str_extract(eventID, "//d+") |>
      as.integer(),
    Latitude = as.numeric(decimalLatitude),
    Longitude = as.numeric(decimalLongitude),
    Start.time = eventTime,
    Sampling.protocol = samplingProtocol,
    Observer = recordedBy,
    Status = identificationVerificationStatus
  ) |>
  filter(
    !is.na(Latitude),
    !is.na(Longitude)
  ) |>
  select("OccuranceID", 
           "Species_name", 
           "Number.of.individuals", 
           "Site.ID", 
           "Checklist.ID",
           "Latitude",
           "Longitude",
           #"Distance.Traveled",
           "year",  
           "month",
           #"Date.Observed",
           "Start.time",
           #"start_hour",
           #"Duration",
           "Sampling.protocol",
           "Party.Size",
           #"Basis.of.record",
           "Observer",
           "Status",
           "Checklist.Completed"
    )

write.csv(sf_data1, "C:/Users/maria/Documents/PhD/data/species/records_clean_gbif.csv")
# Check structure
str(sf_data1)

all_data |>
  count(eventID, sort = TRUE)

```


```{r, echo=FALSE}

obs_tbl <- sf_data1 |>
  group_by(Observer) |>
  summarise(
    checklist_count = n_distinct(Checklist.ID),
    species_count   = n_distinct(Species_name),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    logC = log1p(checklist_count),
    logS = log1p(species_count),
    # standardize to mean 0 sd 1 for Stan stability
    logC_z = as.numeric(scale(logC)),
    logS_z = as.numeric(scale(logS))
  )

write.csv(obs_tbl, "C:/Users/maria/Documents/PhD/data/species/Observers_gbif.csv")
```

For each variable:

Number of observations

Number and proportion of NA

For numerics: mean, sd, min, max, quantiles

For characters/factors: number of unique values


```{r, echo=FALSE}
skim(sf_data1)

```


```{r, echo=FALSE}
num_vars <- sf_data1 |>
  select(where(is.numeric)) |>
  names()

num_vars


sf_data1 |>
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(num_vars),
               names_to = "variable",
               values_to = "value") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Distributions of numeric variables",
    x = "Value",
    y = "Count"
  )



```




```{r, echo=FALSE}
cat_vars <- sf_data1 |>
  select(where(is.character)) |>
  names()

cat_vars

sf_data1 |>
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(cat_vars),
               names_to = "variable",
               values_to = "value") |>
  filter(!is.na(value)) |>
  group_by(variable, value) |>
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  group_by(variable) |>
  slice_max(n, n = 10) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(value, n), y = n)) +
  geom_col(fill = "darkgreen") +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Category",
    y = "Count",
    title = "Top categories per categorical variable"
  )


```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
unique(sf_data1$Sampling.protocol)

unique(sf_data1$Status)
sf_data1$Distance.Traveled

```


```{r, echo=FALSE}
sf_data1 |>
  #mutate(year = year(Date.Observed)) |>
  filter(year >= 2000)|>
  ggplot(aes(x = year)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Number of records per year",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Number of records"
  )



```




```{r, echo=FALSE}
sf_data1 |>
  #mutate(month = month(Date.Observed, label = TRUE)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = month)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Seasonal distribution of observations",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Number of records"
  )
```




```{r, echo=FALSE}
library(tidyr)
sp <- "Danaus plexippus"

dat_df <- as.data.frame(sf_data1)
occ_table <- dat |>
  st_drop_geometry()|>
  group_by(name, month, occ) |>
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  complete(
    name,
    month = 1:12,
    occ = c(0, 1),
    fill = list(n = 0)
  ) |>
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = occ,
    values_from = n,
    names_prefix = "occ_"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    total = occ_0 + occ_1,
    prop_presence = ifelse(total > 0, occ_1 / total, NA_real_),
    prop_absence  = ifelse(total > 0, occ_0 / total, NA_real_)
  ) |>
  arrange(name, month)
occ_table
write.csv(occ_table, "C:/Users/maria/Documents/PhD/data/species/plexippus_PA_count_gbif.csv")

```





















Is the species’ range well sampled or patchy?
Spatial sampling density (kernel density)
A quick way to visualize where sampling effort is concentrated.

Well sampled: smooth density gradients, low Moran’s I, thinning removes few points.

Patchy: clusters around cities, roads, protected areas; high Moran’s I; thinning removes many points.
```{r, echo=TRUE}
library(MASS)

# Hexbin map
ggplot(data_filt, aes(decimalLongitude, decimalLatitude)) +
  stat_bin_hex(bins = 40) +
  scale_fill_viridis() +
  coord_sf() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Spatial sampling density")

# Kernel density
library(MASS)
coords <- data_filt[, c("decimalLongitude", "decimalLatitude")]
kde <- kde2d(coords$decimalLongitude, coords$decimalLatitude, n = 200)

# Spatial autocorrelation
library(spdep)
coords_mat <- as.matrix(coords)
nb <- knn2nb(knearneigh(coords_mat, k = 4))
lw <- nb2listw(nb, style = "W")
moran.test(rep(1, nrow(coords_mat)), lw)


```


Bias grid for SDMs (optional)
Useful if you plan to use Maxent or other presence‑only models.
```{r, echo=TRUE}
library(dismo)

bias_raster <- rasterize(
  data_filt[, c("decimalLongitude", "decimalLatitude")],
  bio[[1]],
  fun = "count"
)

bias_raster <- focal(bias_raster, w = matrix(1, 5, 5), fun = mean, na.rm = TRUE)

plot(bias_raster, main = "Bias grid (sampling effort)")
points(dat_spatial[, c("decimalLongitude", "decimalLatitude")], pch = 20)


```


Are there clear biogeographic boundaries?
Clear boundaries: occurrences fall sharply within certain ecoregions or biomes.

Diffuse boundaries: occurrences span multiple ecoregions without clear separation.

Gaps may reflect either real biogeographic breaks or sampling gaps — compare with sampling density maps.
```{r, echo=TRUE}
# EOO / AOO
library(ConR)
coords_simple <- data_filt[, c("decimalLongitude", "decimalLatitude")]
EOO_AOO(coords_simple)

# Overlay with ecoregions
library(rnaturalearth)
ecoreg <- ne_download(scale = 50, type = "ecoregions", returnclass = "sf")




```


Does sampling bias overwhelm ecological signal?

Bias overwhelms ecology if:

Most points are near cities/roads.

Bias raster strongly predicts occurrence patterns.

Environmental PCA shows clusters aligned with sampling hotspots.

Ecological signal dominates if:

Environmental gradients explain more variation than sampling density.

Thinning does not drastically reduce sample size.
```{r, echo=TRUE}
# Distance to nearest city
library(rnaturalearth)
cities <- ne_download(scale = 50, type = "populated_places", returnclass = "sf")

dist_city <- st_distance(pts, cities)
dat_spatial$dist_to_city_km <- apply(dist_city, 1, min) / 1000

ggplot(dat_spatial, aes(dist_to_city_km)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, fill = "darkorange") +
  theme_minimal()

# Bias raster
bias_raster <- rasterize(
  dat_spatial[, c("decimalLongitude", "decimalLatitude")],
  bio[[1]],
  fun = "count"
)
bias_raster <- focal(bias_raster, w = matrix(1, 5, 5), fun = mean, na.rm = TRUE)
plot(bias_raster)


```


Do environmental variables show clear niche structure?
Clear niche structure:

Tight clusters in PCA space

Strong correlations with specific climate variables

Distinct environmental envelope compared to background

Weak niche structure:

Points spread widely across environmental gradients

No clear clustering in PCA

Environmental outliers dominate
```{r, echo=TRUE}
# PCA
env_vars <- dat_env %>% select(starts_with("bio"))
pca <- prcomp(na.omit(env_vars), scale. = TRUE)

plot(pca$x[,1], pca$x[,2],
     col = viridis(100)[cut(pca$x[,1], 100)],
     pch = 16,
     xlab = "PC1", ylab = "PC2")

# Pairwise plots
pairs(env_vars[,1:5], pch = 16, col = "#00000020")



```
- Is the species’ range well sampled or patchy?

- Are there clear biogeographic boundaries?

- Does sampling bias overwhelm ecological signal?

- Are there temporal trends that might confound modeling?

- Do environmental variables show clear niche structure?

## **5. Environmental Space Analyses**

Extract climate values
PCA of environmental space
```{r, echo=TRUE}
pts <- st_as_sf(dat_spatial,
                coords = c("decimalLongitude", "decimalLatitude"),
                crs = 4326)

env <- raster::extract(bio, pts)
dat_env <- cbind(dat_spatial, env)


env_vars <- dat_env %>% select(starts_with("bio"))
pca <- prcomp(na.omit(env_vars), scale. = TRUE)

plot(pca$x[,1], pca$x[,2],
     col = viridis(100)[cut(pca$x[,1], 100)],
     pch = 16,
     xlab = "PC1", ylab = "PC2",
     main = "Environmental PCA")


```
